commit.message:
fix(pointcloud): exponential backoff on unreachable backend + status banner

When ?backend=<url> pointed at a server that wasn't running (e.g. user
forgot to start ruview-pointcloud serve before clicking Connect ESP32),
the viewer was retrying 10 Hz forever — flooding the console with
ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED and offering no guidance about what was wrong.

Two fixes:

1. Replace setInterval(fetchCloud, 100) with self-rescheduling
   setTimeout. On success: 250 ms steady cadence. On failure for an
   explicit backend: 250 ms → 500 → 1 s → 2 s → 4 s → 8 s → 16 s →
   capped at 30 s. Resets to 250 ms the moment the backend comes back.
   Auto mode (Pages with no backend) still disables network entirely
   after the first 404. Strict-live mode (?live=1) also backs off so
   it doesn't spam.

2. Show an actionable status banner in the info panel when the chosen
   backend is unreachable: the URL, the actual error string, the next
   retry time, and the exact `cargo run` command to start the server.
   Visitor sees the diagnosis instead of staring at a 'demo' badge
   wondering why their ESP32 feed isn't visible.

The scene keeps animating (face mesh / synthetic) while the viewer
waits, so the tab never goes blank.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

feat(pointcloud): overlay browser face mesh on top of ESP32 backend feed

Lets the visitor enable their browser webcam face mesh in addition to
(not instead of) a connected ESP32 backend. Both render in the same
Three.js scene — the live ESP32-driven splats from /api/splats plus the
visitor's own face as a 478-vertex MediaPipe point cloud. Use cases:

- Local development: see your face overlaid on the camera+CSI fusion
  output to debug coordinate-frame alignment.
- Demos: show 'this is the room as ESP32 sees it, and this is me as
  MediaPipe sees me' side-by-side in one scene.

Implementation:
- Extract pushFaceSplats(splats) — pushes the 478 face vertices plus
  ~8000 edge-interpolated samples into the array, with no Foundation
  context. Reused by faceMeshFrame (demo path) and handleData (overlay
  path) so there is one source of truth for face-splat geometry.
- handleData now appends pushFaceSplats output to data.splats when the
  source is not 'face-mesh' AND the user has clicked the camera CTA.
  Sets data._faceOverlay so the badge can show '+ face overlay'.
- Camera CTA is no longer hidden in remote/live modes — it relabels to
  '▶ Add face overlay' so the affordance is clear. Strict-live mode
  (?live=1) still hides it because the offline panel takes over.
- Splat count in the info panel reflects the rendered total (backend +
  overlay) when the overlay is active.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

feat(pointcloud): integrate ESP32 CSI as optional data stream from hosted viewer

The hosted GitHub Pages viewer can now act as a thin client for a
locally-running ruview-pointcloud serve instance — flip a button, the
ESP32's CSI fusion (camera depth + WiFi CSI + mmWave) renders inside
the same Three.js scene that previously only showed the face mesh
demo. No clone, no rebuild, no toolchain on the visitor's side.

Server (stream.rs):
- Add tower_http::cors::CorsLayer with a deliberate allowlist:
  https://ruvnet.github.io, http://localhost:*, http://127.0.0.1:*,
  and 'null' (for file:// origins). Anything else is denied — not a
  wildcard CORS. Modern browsers (Chrome 94+, Firefox 116+, Safari
  16.4+) treat 127.0.0.1 as a "potentially trustworthy" origin so
  HTTPS Pages → HTTP loopback is permitted. The new layer wraps the
  existing /api/cloud, /api/splats, /api/status, /health routes.
- Cargo.toml: pull in workspace tower-http (cors feature already on).

Viewer:
- New "📡 Connect ESP32…" CTA bottom-right. Clicking prompts for a
  ruview-pointcloud serve URL (default http://127.0.0.1:9880),
  persists the last-used value in localStorage, and reloads with
  ?backend=<url> so the existing remote-mode fetch path takes over.
  When already connected the button toggles to "disconnect" and
  reloads back to the demo.
- Reuses the existing transport selector — no new code path to
  maintain. The face mesh / synthetic demo render path is unaffected;
  this is purely an additive UI affordance over the ?backend= query.

Docs:
- ADR-094 §2.3 expanded with the local-ESP32 workflow and the CORS
  posture rationale.
- Workflow README documents ?backend=http://127.0.0.1:9880 as the
  intended local-ESP32 path.

Tests: cargo test -p wifi-densepose-pointcloud → 15/15 passed.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

chore(pointcloud): inline amber-dot favicon to silence /favicon.ico 404

Browsers auto-request /favicon.ico when none is declared in <head>.
On a static GitHub Pages host that's a guaranteed 404 in the console.
Inline a 32x32 SVG amber dot via data: URL so the browser is satisfied
without an extra network round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

fix(pointcloud): stop polling /api/splats on Pages after first 404

When the viewer is hosted on a static origin (GitHub Pages, S3) it has
no backend at /api/splats. The default ?backend=auto path was issuing
a fetch every 100 ms, getting a 404, falling back to the demo, and
flooding the console with one 404 per tick. Cosmetic on the surface
but real network/CPU waste over time.

After the first 404 in auto mode, set networkDisabled=true and skip
fetch on subsequent ticks — the interval still fires but goes straight
to pickDemoFrame() so the face mesh / synthetic render path keeps
animating. Remote (?backend=<url>) and live (?live=1) modes keep
retrying so a transient outage doesn't permanently downgrade them.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

Revert "feat(pointcloud): Hollywood face fx — webcam texture, wireframe, scan line"

This reverts commit 347ad4bb116a9f31f6939541666c48646c0b4a5d.

feat(pointcloud): Hollywood face fx — webcam texture, wireframe, scan line

Adds optional cinematic effects to the face-mesh demo, all toggleable
via a new ?fx= URL param. Default is 'all' (texture + mesh + scan +
halo). Lightweight modes available: ?fx=clean (texture only) or
?fx=points (original solid amber).

- Texture: per-frame webcam → hidden 2D canvas → getImageData lookup
  at each landmark (and each interpolated edge sample). Splats now
  carry the visitor's actual skin tone, not solid amber. Sampling is
  mirrored on x to match the selfie convention used by the face mesh
  vertex placement. All on-device — no frames leave the browser.
- Mesh: persistent THREE.LineSegments overlay drawn from
  FACEMESH_TESSELATION (~1300 edges). Translucent (opacity 0.35),
  amber, additive blending, depthWrite off — gives a holographic
  wireframe wrapping the point cloud. Geometry is updated in place
  each frame; only positions get re-uploaded.
- Scan: vertical bright slab sweeps top→bottom every 4 seconds,
  amplifying splat color up to 2.6× when within ±0.08 world units of
  the line. Westworld-style scanning.
- Halo: existing 60-particle ring around the face is now opt-in via
  FX_HALO. Cleaner default for the texture-mesh combination.

Info panel surfaces active fx list in face-mesh mode. Synthetic
fallback hides the wireframe overlay so it doesn't render against an
empty figure. Workflow README updated with the new ?fx= options.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

ci: add cog PR validation workflow (#9)

Auto-validates every PR that touches a cog or shared crate. Stops new
cogs from landing without an ADR or with broken manifests.

Jobs:

1. changed-cogs — diff against PR base; emits a JSON list of cogs to
   check + a flag for shared-crate changes.

2. check — matrix over changed cogs (max-parallel 6), runs
   `cargo check --release --all-targets` and `cargo test --release`
   per cog. If a shared crate or root Cargo changed, the matrix
   expands to all cogs.

3. shared-crates — `cargo check` + `cargo test` on
   crates/cog-sensor-sources when it (or root Cargo) changes.

4. manifest-validate — for every cog: cog.toml + Cargo.toml +
   src/main.rs must exist; id in cog.toml must match directory name;
   [[bin]] name in Cargo.toml must be `cog-<dir>`. Catches the
   parking-occupancy / borrow-check style regressions earlier.

5. adr-required — for new cogs (added cog.toml in PR), require a
   matching ADR in docs/adrs/. Stops un-documented cogs from landing.

Cache: per-cog target dir keyed by Cargo.toml hash + shared crate.

commit.message:
feat: 15 new cogs (ADR-002..016) + RuView optional capabilities (#8)

* feat(health-monitor): self-contained sensor sources (--source esp32-uart / esp32-udp) — v1.1.0

Implements ADR-091 Phase 1. Adds --source <kind>[=<param>] flag so
the cog can read directly from the ESP32 instead of going through the
agent's sensor/stream aggregator (which returns synthetic data on
default_synthetic seeds).

Sources:
  - seed-stream            (default, backward compat with v1.0.0)
  - esp32-uart=<path>      ESP32 serial, parses csi_collector RSSI;
                           --features esp32-uart
  - esp32-udp=<bind_addr>  ADR-069 0xC5110003 feature packets;
                           --features esp32-udp

DSP pipeline unchanged — only the data source plugs differently.

Validated on Windows with ESP32 esp32-csi-node v0.6.1 on COM8:
  $ cog-health-monitor.exe --once --source esp32-uart=COM8 --window-ms 15000
    {"signal_variance":0.196,...}
  Subsequent runs: 0.226, 0.003, 0.0003 — real RSSI fluctuation, not
  the quantized {50,100,150,200} pattern from zero-crossings on
  default_synthetic noise.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* feat(ruview-densepose): self-contained sensor sources (--source esp32-uart / esp32-udp) — v1.1.0

Implements ADR-091 Phase 1 for the second DSP cog. Adds the same
--source <kind>[=<param>] pattern as cog-health-monitor v1.1.0:
  - auto                    (default — preserves v1.0.0 fallback behavior:
                             try UDP :5005 first, fall back to seed-stream)
  - seed-stream             agent's /api/v1/sensor/stream only
  - esp32-uart=<path>       direct serial-port reader; --features esp32-uart
  - esp32-udp=<host:port>   explicit ADR-069 UDP listener; --features esp32-udp

The Hampel filter, phase sanitization, and 17-keypoint mapping pipeline
is unchanged — only the data source plugs differently.

cog.toml version synced to 1.1.0 (was 0.5.0, drifted from Cargo.toml).

Validated on Windows host with ESP32 esp32-csi-node v0.6.1 on COM8:
  $ cog-ruview-densepose.exe --once --source esp32-uart=COM8 --window-ms 15000
    Run 1: 17 keypoints, avg_confidence=0.176, raw_features=5, source=esp32-uart
    Run 2: 17 keypoints, avg_confidence=0.122, raw_features=4, source=esp32-uart
  Both metrics vary across runs — real ESP32 packet-rate fluctuation, not
  the static signature of synthetic input.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* feat(cogs): unify default --source=auto, prefer ESP32 UDP :5006 over WiFi

Both cog-health-monitor and cog-ruview-densepose now default to
'auto' mode: a short UDP probe on the ADR-069 standard port 5006,
with fallback to the agent's sensor/stream if no packets arrive.
This means dropping a cog onto a fleet seed that's already on a
WiFi network with an ESP32 unicasting CSI features Just Works
without --source flags or per-deploy config.

Changes per cog:
  health-monitor v1.1.0 -> v1.2.0:
    - new Source::Auto variant (UDP-first, ~800ms probe, fallback)
    - default --source = "auto" (was "seed-stream")
    - source_tag in HealthReport now reads "auto:esp32-udp" /
      "auto:seed-stream" so operators can see which path won

  ruview-densepose v1.1.0 -> v1.2.0:
    - UDP_PORT 5005 -> 5006 (legacy CSI broadcast -> ADR-069)
    - cog.toml + Cargo.toml versions synced to 1.2.0

Backward compat: every named source from v1.0.0 / v1.1.0 still
parses ('seed-stream', 'esp32-uart=PATH', 'esp32-udp=BIND'), and
'auto' on a seed without a UDP sender behaves identically to the
old 'seed-stream' default.

ADR-091 amendment: the bridge in seed/scripts/esp32-uart-to-udp-bridge.py
is no longer the on-WiFi path; it stays as the Pi-Zero-USB-host
workaround. On any seed with WiFi access to the ESP32 (the common
case), --source=auto is enough.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* feat(cogs): migrate all 88 cogs to shared cog-sensor-sources crate (ADR-091 phase 3)

Adds a small shared crate (crates/cog-sensor-sources/) that owns the
self-contained sensor source logic — UDP probe at :5006 (ADR-069
MAGIC_FEATURES) for 2s, fall back to /api/v1/sensor/stream — and
returns the same {"samples": [...]} JSON shape every cog already
expected. Each cog's fetch_sensors() body becomes a one-liner:

    fn fetch_sensors() -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
        cog_sensor_sources::fetch_sensors()
    }

Migration tool (scripts/migrate-cog-to-shared-sources.py) does the
mechanical changes idempotently and string/comment-aware so it
doesn't trip on format!("{e}") braces. Every cog with a fetch_sensors
function in the registry now goes through the shared crate (88 cogs
total, 80+ touched here, 2 already on v1.2.0 from earlier PRs).

This means dropping any cog onto a fleet seed already on a WiFi
network with an ESP32 unicasting CSI features Just Works — no per-cog
flag tweaks, no agent-side reconfig.

Sample-built 8 representative cogs across categories (DSP, swarm,
networking) — all compile clean with one warning each at most.

Bulk ARM cross-build + GCS upload + registry bump are scheduled in
the next /loop iteration.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* chore: gitignore dist/ build output dir

* feat: 15 new cogs (ADR-002..016) + cog-sensor-sources COG_SENSOR_URL

Adds 15 practical-capability cogs across health / security / building /
retail / industrial / agriculture domains, each with a dedicated ADR:

  Health:    fall-detect, cough-detect, baby-cry, snore-monitor
  Security:  glass-break, gunshot-detect
  Retail:    package-detect, parking-occupancy
  Industrial: ppe-compliance, slip-fall-zone, predictive-maintenance
  Building:  water-leak, smoke-fire, frost-warning, beehive-monitor

Optional ruvnet/ruview integration:
  - --ruview-mode flag on: fall-detect, gunshot-detect, slip-fall-zone, smoke-fire
  - Required: package-detect, ppe-compliance, parking-occupancy
  - None: cough-detect, baby-cry, snore-monitor, glass-break, water-leak,
    frost-warning, beehive-monitor, predictive-maintenance

ADRs at docs/adrs/ADR-001..016 + RUVIEW-CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md +
README.md index.

Validated end-to-end on live seed (firmware 0.21.8 at 169.254.42.1):
  - All 15 cogs cross-compile to armhf (~411-462 KB stripped)
  - All 15 install via POST /api/v1/apps/install (cloud registry)
  - All 15 produce well-formed JSON via /console
  - --ruview-mode validated: fall-detect, gunshot-detect, slip-fall-zone, smoke-fire
  - 3 running concurrently on seed: fall-detect, cough-detect, smoke-fire

cog-sensor-sources crate: added COG_SENSOR_URL env var support so cogs
can be exercised against a remote seed during dev (defaults to 127.0.0.1:80
loopback when unset).

Dockerfile.cog-batch: single-cog cross-compile recipe; build-all-arm.sh
already iterates over src/cogs/ so it picks these up automatically.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

feat: 15 new cogs (ADR-002..016) + RuView optional capabilities (#8)

* feat(health-monitor): self-contained sensor sources (--source esp32-uart / esp32-udp) — v1.1.0

Implements ADR-091 Phase 1. Adds --source <kind>[=<param>] flag so
the cog can read directly from the ESP32 instead of going through the
agent's sensor/stream aggregator (which returns synthetic data on
default_synthetic seeds).

Sources:
  - seed-stream            (default, backward compat with v1.0.0)
  - esp32-uart=<path>      ESP32 serial, parses csi_collector RSSI;
                           --features esp32-uart
  - esp32-udp=<bind_addr>  ADR-069 0xC5110003 feature packets;
                           --features esp32-udp

DSP pipeline unchanged — only the data source plugs differently.

Validated on Windows with ESP32 esp32-csi-node v0.6.1 on COM8:
  $ cog-health-monitor.exe --once --source esp32-uart=COM8 --window-ms 15000
    {"signal_variance":0.196,...}
  Subsequent runs: 0.226, 0.003, 0.0003 — real RSSI fluctuation, not
  the quantized {50,100,150,200} pattern from zero-crossings on
  default_synthetic noise.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* feat(ruview-densepose): self-contained sensor sources (--source esp32-uart / esp32-udp) — v1.1.0

Implements ADR-091 Phase 1 for the second DSP cog. Adds the same
--source <kind>[=<param>] pattern as cog-health-monitor v1.1.0:
  - auto                    (default — preserves v1.0.0 fallback behavior:
                             try UDP :5005 first, fall back to seed-stream)
  - seed-stream             agent's /api/v1/sensor/stream only
  - esp32-uart=<path>       direct serial-port reader; --features esp32-uart
  - esp32-udp=<host:port>   explicit ADR-069 UDP listener; --features esp32-udp

The Hampel filter, phase sanitization, and 17-keypoint mapping pipeline
is unchanged — only the data source plugs differently.

cog.toml version synced to 1.1.0 (was 0.5.0, drifted from Cargo.toml).

commit.message:
docs(plugins): add plugins/README.md

Documents the 103 cog plugins under plugins/, the uniform slash command
surface, the "what runs where" architecture, the 10 categories with
counts/examples, the RuView mode matrix (optional/required/none), how
to test a single plugin via claude --plugin-dir, and how to regenerate
plugins from the cog registry.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

feat: 103 cog plugins + per-cog ADRs + cog-dev plugin (#1)

* feat: 103 cog plugins + per-cog ADRs + cog-dev plugin

Adds one Claude Code plugin per cog in cognitum-one/cogs registry, plus the
cog-dev workflow plugin. Each cog plugin contains:

  cogs/<id>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json   — manifest
  cogs/<id>/commands/<id>.md             — slash command (/cog-<id>)
  cogs/<id>/docs/ADR-<id>.md             — per-cog architecture decision

Slash command wraps the seed's cog management endpoints:
  /<id>            install if needed, start, tail logs
  /<id> --once     one-shot via /console
  /<id> --console  pass arbitrary args (e.g. --ruview-mode)
  /<id> --stop     stop on seed
  /<id> --logs     recent stdout/stderr

Per-cog ADRs were authored by an 8-agent team in parallel, each tailored
to that cog's signal-processing approach (DTW, FFT, autoencoder, EWC,
Welford z-score, autocorrelation, etc.) with honest framing of research
cogs and explicit RuView mode treatment for the 12 newer health/security
cogs (5 optional, 3 required).

cog-dev/ — separate workflow plugin with 5 slash commands (/cog-new,
/cog-test, /cog-deploy, /cog-trace, /cog-config), 5 skills, and 4
subagents covering author / test / deploy / ops loops.

Validated locally via:
  claude --plugin-dir cogs/<id>     # loads plugin
  /cog-<id>:<id> --logs              # tails live cog output from seed

End-to-end run on live seed (firmware 0.21.8) confirmed:
  fall-detect z_impact trending 2 -> 3.8 (below 6.0 threshold)
  cough-detect spectral_ratio 4.98
  smoke-fire signals_active 0/3 in quiet env

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* refactor: rename cogs/ to plugins/ to match Claude Code marketplace convention

Path is now `plugins/<cog-id>/` instead of `cogs/<cog-id>/`. The plugin
name (`cog-<id>`) is unchanged. Marketplace.json source.path entries
will need to follow in the dashboard mirror.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

* docs(README): expand to cover all 106 plugins (3 base + 103 cogs)

Replaces the previous cognitum-mcp-only README with a full marketplace
overview: repo layout, the 3 platform plugins (cognitum-mcp, cognitum-seed,
cog-dev), the 103 cog plugins by category, ruvnet/ruview integration
matrix, per-plugin doc layout, and how to test a single plugin via
claude --plugin-dir without registering the marketplace.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
